commit.message:
Add external-links-policy.md — single source of truth for link/citation rules

New seo-guidelines doc covering the full citation enforcement system:

- The one-sentence rule + rationale (with real hallucination examples)
- 4 enforcement layers: AI prompt, validate_outbound_links() passes,
  Content_Injector citation injector, check-citations Claude skill
- Full trusted domain whitelist with categories
- How research APIs feed URLs (and why the AI still can't cite them directly)
- 6 known failure modes with detection + expected result
- Manual and automated testing procedures
- Version history 1.4.x → 1.5.7

This file is now the contract. Any change to validate_outbound_links,
the URL system prompt, inject_citations, or the whitelist must update
this doc in the same commit.

commit.message:
v1.5.7 — Fix title selection, Save Draft nonce error, hallucinated References section

Three bugs reported from Test 1 (how-to article generation):

1. Title selection broken — radio buttons had onchange='document.getElementById
   ("async-draft-title").value=this.value' but async-draft-title doesn't
   exist in the DOM, throwing Cannot set properties of null on every click.
   Removed the broken onchange; the :checked query already works natively.
   Also escape quotes in the value attribute to prevent markup breakage.

2. 'The link you followed has expired' on Save Draft — the seobetter-async-
   result div was inside the outer <form method='post'>, so clicks on the
   JS-injected Save Draft button were bubbling into a form submission under
   some conditions (despite type='button'). Moved the async result div
   outside the form entirely. Also added explicit e.preventDefault() and
   e.stopPropagation() to the Save Draft click handler as belt-and-braces.

3. Hallucinated References section — the validator was stripping individual
   links inline but leaving numbered References entries with dead text like
   '1. Dog Facts API — source'. Added sanitize_references_section() that
   runs before the inline link pass: walks each line of the References/
   Sources/Bibliography section, checks every link against the full
   whitelist rules (anchor text, URL path, host, deep-link, domain), drops
   any line that fails, and removes the heading entirely if no references
   survive. Also handles list items with no link at all (drops them — a
   citation with no URL has no value).

System prompt now explicitly tells the AI not to generate a References
section at all — 'These sections are processed automatically and will be
stripped if they contain hallucinated URLs. Use inline plain-text
attributions throughout the article body instead.'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redo PostHog setup following official Next.js 15.3+ docs

- Use instrumentation-client.ts for PostHog init (recommended for Next.js 16)
- Simplify providers.tsx to just a React context wrapper
- Route events through /ingest reverse proxy to bypass blockers
- Use NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY env var name consistently

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Vercel build crash — remove throw on missing env vars

The hard throw in supabase.js caused builds to fail on Vercel since
NEXT_PUBLIC_* env vars aren't available at module evaluation time during
static generation. Replaced with empty string fallback so build succeeds;
runtime requests will still fail gracefully if vars are missing.

or cancel) so the user always returns to the current hour.

commit.message:
release: v1.8.0 — LLM Wiki support (get_backlinks, get_stale_pages, get_wiki_stats)

Three new tools for the LLM Wiki pattern (a la Karpathy):
- get_backlinks: inverse reference graph — find all pages linking to a target doc
- get_stale_pages: frontmatter-based source provenance and staleness detection
- get_wiki_stats: wiki health dashboard (orphans, most-linked, tags, link density)

384 tests, 0 failures.

fix: upgrade Lucide to 0.460.0 in app + add CDN fallback

Lucide 0.344.0 returns 503 on jsdelivr, causing app crash
(ReferenceError: lucide is not defined on renderDashboard).
Upgraded to 0.460.0 (same as landing) and added no-op fallback
so the app works even if CDN is temporarily down.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

template hardening: 8 fixes from comprehensive audit

CRITICAL fixes:
1. CLAUDE.md: full rewrite — was massively outdated (old file structure,
   old Caddyfile approach, old secrets list). Now accurately reflects
   the current architecture, deploy flow, backup system, and migration
   framework.

2. backup/Dockerfile: mc binary download now verified with SHA256
   checksum. Prevents supply chain attacks if dl.min.io is compromised.

3. docker-compose.yml: healthcheck timeout 5s → 3s (was equal to the
   5s interval, causing overlapping probes under load).

4. scripts/swarm-setup.sh: worker join now retries 5 times with backoff
   instead of silently masking failures with `|| true`.

HIGH fixes:
5. All 3 Dockerfiles: added OCI labels (image.source, image.description)
   for provenance tracking — can now trace which repo built which image.

6. scripts/restore-from-backup.sh: replaced fragile eval-based credential
   mapping with clean fallback: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID ← BACKUP_S3_ACCESS_KEY.

7. patroni/etcd/haproxy stack files: added max_attempts: 10, window: 120s
   to all restart_policy blocks. A crash-looping container now stops after
   10 attempts in 2 minutes instead of restarting infinitely.

commit.message:
v1.5.6 — Only allow direct-source article links (no homepages, no API anchor text)

User directive: 'never link to an api, or a made up URL, only ever link to
the source of the reference in the article the direct article source, if it
doesnt exist dont link it and dont link with words like Dog Facts API ever.'

Rules now enforced by validate_outbound_links() (article save path) AND
Content_Injector::inject_citations() (Fix Now button):

1. Anchor text cannot contain 'api', 'endpoint', 'dataset', 'sdk', 'webhook'
   — strips [Dog Facts API](...), [Pexels API](...), [Reddit API](...) etc.
2. URL path cannot contain /api/, /v1/, /v2/, /graphql, /rest/, /swagger
3. URL host cannot match api.*, *-api.*, *.herokuapp.com
4. URL must be a DEEP link — bare homepages like rspca.org.au/ are now
   stripped. Citations must point to a specific article, study, or page.
5. Citation injector also rejects sources with missing/generic titles
   ('Source', 'Article', 'Untitled', <8 chars)

System prompt rewritten to match: 'The ONLY legitimate hyperlink is one
that points to the SPECIFIC article/study/page you are citing. Not a
homepage. Not a category page. Not an API.' Plus: 'an article with good
plain-text attributions and zero hyperlinks is a pass. 5 hyperlinks to
homepages or APIs is a FAIL.'

Plain-text citations like 'According to a 2026 RSPCA report' remain
encouraged and count equally for GEO visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.5.5 — Strict link whitelist: strip all non-authoritative outbound URLs

User reported articles containing hallucinated URLs: dog-facts-api.herokuapp.com,
dog-api.kinduff.com/api/facts, and mindiampets.com.au/wp-admin/Dog%20Facts%20API
(where AI output literal text in the URL slot of a markdown link).

Three-layer fix:

1. validate_outbound_links() rewritten as a strict whitelist:
   - Pass 1: strip malformed markdown links where the URL isn't http(s)://
     (catches [Dog Facts API](Dog Facts API) → 'Dog Facts API' plain text)
   - Pass 2: only keep links to trusted domains (*.gov, *.edu, wikipedia.org,
     rspca.org.au, akc.org, who.int, cdc.gov, etc.) — everything else unlinked
   - No more HEAD-request fallback logic (dead domains used to become dead
     homepage links — gone)
   - Extensible via 'seobetter_trusted_domains' filter

2. System prompt rewritten with ZERO-TOLERANCE URL rules:
   - DO NOT invent URL paths
   - DO NOT link to API endpoints or dev hosts (*.herokuapp.com etc)
   - DO NOT use link text as URL (malformed markdown)
   - Plain-text citations ('According to the RSPCA') count equally for GEO
   - When in doubt, OMIT the link entirely

3. Content_Injector::inject_citations now HEAD-checks every source URL before
   adding it and rejects API endpoints outright. If zero URLs pass the live
   check, the injection fails loudly rather than emitting dead citations.

Also wired validate_outbound_links into the inject-fix path so citation
injection can't reintroduce bad links after the initial sanitization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix self-analysis findings: security suppressions, complexity reduction, --exclude, npm-audit parser

Ran code-explorer on itself and fixed everything it found:

- Add nosec/nosemgrep inline suppressions for intentional chmod 0o755 and
  trusted xml.etree usage (with correct full rule IDs)
- Reduce cyclomatic complexity across 6 parsers, 2 generators, and both
  templates by extracting helpers (e.g. parse_lighthouse_a11y CCN 61→19)
- Add --exclude flag to generated analyzers (defaults: generated,
  node_modules, dist, .git) so self-analysis focuses on source code
- Fix parse_generic to skip stack trace frames (at ..., node_modules/)
- Add parse_npm_audit.py for proper JSON parsing instead of generic fallback

fix: load kiwix DLL after entering alternate screen

The DLL's DllMain calls freopen("NUL", "w", stdout) which kills
the process stdout. Moving the DLL load to after crossterm's
EnterAlternateScreen ensures crossterm has already claimed the
Win32 console handle before the C stdout redirect happens.

commit.message:
refactor(execution): decouple MemoryArriveKind from trace layer

Phase 4 of architecture restructure - remove trace dependency from
shared execution state.

- Create new MemoryArriveKind enum in execution/internal/memory_arrive_kind.h
- Update PendingMemoryOp in wave_state.h to use MemoryArriveKind
- Add ToTraceMemoryArriveKind() conversion in event_factory.h
- Update functional_exec_engine.cpp and program_object_exec_engine.cpp
  to use MemoryArriveKind internally and convert at trace boundary

This resolves V4 layering violation: execution state no longer depends
on trace layer types. The trace layer now consumes execution types
through explicit conversion.

liveblog, and recipe (which have their own structured data).

commit.message:
feat: Environmental Management module — NEMA/NWA/NEM:AQA

Closes the HIGH-priority gap identified in enterprise analysis.
Adds a dedicated /environment dashboard page covering three pillars:

Permits & Licences
- EnvironmentalPermit model (CEMP, WUL, AEL, Waste Licence, EIA Authorisation)
- 4-step creation wizard with type, dates, assignment, and conditions
- Status lifecycle: Draft → Submitted → Active → Expired / Revoked
- Configurable renewal lead days with colour-coded expiry warnings

Aspects & Impacts Register
- EnvironmentalAspect model (activity → aspect → impact)
- ISO 14001 clause 6.1.2 / NEMA s24 aligned significance rating
- Risk level (Low → Critical) + control measures + legal requirement fields

Monitoring Readings
- EnvironmentalReading model linked to existing MonitoringPoint
- Auto-flags exceedances when value > limit
- Red row highlighting for limit breaches in the readings table

Cron & Notifications
- ENVIRONMENTAL_PERMIT_EXPIRING: daily check auto-expires active permits
  past expiry date and notifies responsible user within renewalLeadDays
- ENVIRONMENTAL_EXCEEDANCE: notification type wired for real-time alerting
- Both types added to notification-preferences settings and email templates

Infrastructure
- Prisma migration: 3 new models, 3 new enums, 2 new NotificationType values
- Sidebar: Environment nav item (Leaf icon) added to core items
- Feature gate: environmentalMonitoring (existing Enterprise gate)
- Zero TypeScript errors, production build passing

closing brace, causing 'SyntaxError: missing ) after argument list' at

sbSuggestTopics was never defined even though the function was declared

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update progress for Phase 3 completion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: agentic executor + retry loop + self-discovering web research

Three capabilities that make the agent truly autonomous:

1. Agentic executor (agent/executor.py):
   ReAct-style multi-step loop where Gemma can use 10 tools:
     READ_FILE, SEARCH_FILES, GREP, RUN_COMMAND, SEARCH_CANON,
     SEARCH_WEB, BROWSE_URL, SEARCH_TRANSCRIPTS, DISCOVER_SOURCES, DONE

   The agent reasons step-by-step (THOUGHT -> ACTION -> OBSERVATION),
   uses tools to gather evidence, handles errors and adjusts approach,
   and iterates until it has enough information to draft a proposal.
   Max 15 iterations with read-only enforcement by default and
   destructive command blocking (rm, push, install, etc.).

   This replaces the previous single-Gemma-call draft loop. The agent
   can now read arbitrary files, search codebases, browse URLs, search
   its own knowledge base, and discover new sources — all in a single
   draft session.

   15 tests covering action parsing, tool execution (file read, glob
   search, command blocking, protected paths), and the full agent loop
   (immediate done, multi-tool, max-iteration safety, no-action-as-answer).

2. Retry with learning (agent/applier.py):
   apply_with_retry() wraps the apply mechanism with up to 3 attempts.
   On each failure, the CI error log is fed back to Gemma as context:
   "Previous attempt failed because... Fix the issues. Do NOT repeat
   the same mistakes." This is the core fail-learn-iterate loop.

3. Self-discovering web research (web_learner.py):
   - discover_sources(query): searches DuckDuckGo for new sources,
     deduplicates against the existing allowlist
   - The draft loop auto-discovers when existing web evidence is thin
   - Discovered sources are flagged as "trust=0.5, not yet allowlisted"
   - Agent can propose adding them via the queue
   - 3 daemon endpoints: /web/search, /web/discover, /web/add-source

The draft loop now runs the full agentic executor instead of a single
Gemma call. The agent can browse documentation, read source code, search
its canon and transcripts, discover new sources, and iterate across
multiple tool calls before producing its proposal.

Tests: 143 -> 158 (+15 executor tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 81 edge case tests from coverage audit, fix constants in conditions

Coverage audit found and fixed a real bug: builtin constants (pi, e, phi)
were not recognized in equation conditions. check_condition() now checks
builtin_constants() before returning "unknown variable", so conditions
like "y = x : x > pi" work correctly.

New test sections:
- Numeric edge cases: Newton at exact root, step functions, zero samples,
  narrow intervals, constant functions
- Constants edge cases: conditions with pi, phi in equations, multiple
  constants in derive, formula call bindings with constants, negative
  constant recognition, near-pi values, override behavior
- Derive edge cases: 3-level deep formula chains, conditions on sub-system
  equations, multiple constants preserved in derive
- Fit edge cases: too few samples, NaN filtering, reciprocal 1/x,
  constant function fit, fraction recognition (0, -0.5, huge, NaN, inf)

1516 tests, all passing under ASan + UBSan.
